patch.value: Round-trip-safe stdout export, documented export flags, multi-snapshot history (--history-dir / --compare <dir>)

pm standup export --format json with no --output emits ONLY the exported document on stdout (valid JSON, no pm result envelope) via a scoped output_format service override with a legacy fallback; pm standup export --help documents every exporter flag; standup export --history-dir <dir> writes a dated standup-YYYY-MM-DD.json snapshot; pm standup --compare <dir> renders trend deltas vs the newest snapshot plus a History count-sequence footer for 2+ snapshots, degrading gracefully for empty/unusable directories; manifest declares manifest_version/trusted/sandbox_profile/permissions + services capability; @unbrained/pm-cli floor 2026.6.11; unit tests for all new helpers; README documents stdout round-trip + history; changelog:check green

value.author: codex
value.text: SALVAGE REVIEW: resumed /tmp/salvage-pm-slack-standup.diff (626 lines, prior session died mid-flight). Verified the approach against pm-cli 2026.6.11 internals before reuse: formatOutput() runs the 'output_format' service override with the full result payload ({command,result,...}); a handler returning a string makes pm print exactly that string; returning the received (cloned) sctx.payload object is treated as not-handled (identity check in runServiceOverrideSync). SDK.md sanctions this ('For output_format, return context.payload/null/undefined for unrelated commands'). Diff applied cleanly to main@3458dbb and is sound — REUSED in full, then extended.

value.author: codex
value.text: DESIGN: (1) stdout fix = scoped output_format service: only when active command is 'standup export' AND the result carries raw_stdout:true does the service return result.output verbatim; every other command/result returns sctx.payload untouched (not handled). When the runtime lacks registerService, exporter falls back to legacy console.log (envelope still appended) — graceful degradation, never silent data loss. (2) export --help fix = ImportExportRegistrationOptions third arg to registerExporter (new SDK surface): description/intent/examples/failure_hints/flags now surface in help + runtime contracts. (3) history: export --history-dir writes <dir>/standup-YYYY-MM-DD.json (always JSON snapshot shape, one per local day); --compare <dir> uses newest snapshot as trend baseline + renders History count-sequence footer (max 8 snapshots, lexicographic=chronological order, per-file warn+skip on junk). (4) manifest: manifest_version 2, trusted, sandbox_profile=restricted, permissions {fs_read,fs_write,network,env_read,process_spawn:true; env_write:false}, +services capability, pm_min_version 2026.6.7.

value.author: codex
value.text: VERIFICATION (throwaway workspace /tmp/pm-enh-comms-2450469/standup, real pm items, built local package installed via pm install <dir> --project): (a) pm standup export --format json > f.json now exits 0 and python -m json.tool accepts stdout (was: 'Extra data' from appended envelope); (b) pm standup --dry-run --compare f.json renders 'Trend vs prior: In Progress ▲+1 …' — documented round-trip restored; (c) pm standup export --help lists --format/--output/--history-dir/--include-done/... (was: no flags); (d) export --history-dir .standup-history wrote standup-2026-06-12.json; with two synthetic older snapshots, --compare .standup-history rendered both the trend line and 'History (3 snapshots → now): In Progress 0→1→2→2 · …' in text + Block Kit footer. package doctor: active=1 failed=0. npm test 67/67 (7 new history tests), build + tsc --noEmit clean.

value.author: codex
value.text: ALSO VERIFIED + CLOSING dc4i: all four acceptance criteria of pm-slack-standup-dc4i already landed on main and pass hands-on: --up-next 1 caps Up Next, --group-by milestone buckets with (no milestone), --since not-a-date warns loudly ('warning: ignoring unparseable --since'), export --output /proc/nope/x.md raises friendly CommandError (no fs stack). No reimplementation needed.

value.author: codex
value.text: 1) pm renders every extension command's return value as a result envelope on stdout; the ONLY sanctioned way for a command to own stdout is an output_format service override (services capability) that returns a string — returning the received sctx.payload object marks it not-handled because the runtime compares result identity against the cloned payload. Scope the override to your own command name AND a result marker (raw_stdout:true) so unrelated commands are untouched. 2) registerExporter/registerImporter accept a third ImportExportRegistrationOptions arg (SDK 2026.6.x) that gives the auto-generated '<name> export' command real --help flags/examples — without it the command surface is invisible to users and contracts. 3) pm install only accepts a package DIRECTORY, not an npm-pack tgz. 4) The exporter's lexicographic standup-YYYY-MM-DD.json naming makes filename sort == chronological sort, which keeps multi-snapshot history dependency-free.

patch.value: Shipped + hands-on verified vs CLI 2026.6.11: stdout JSON export is round-trip valid (output_format service, legacy fallback kept), standup export --help documents all flags (ImportExportRegistrationOptions), --history-dir/--compare <dir> multi-snapshot history, hardened manifest (manifest_version 2/trusted/restricted/permissions/services), 67/67 tests, changelog:check green.
